quote: though it was not available to this project for direct consultation
issue: Meta-commentary about source availability addresses the production process rather than the reader.
suggestion: Remove the clause or rephrase to avoid meta-commentary, e.g., 'The English-language grammar <Ref k="tamura2000" /> made the Saru description accessible to non-Japanese readership.'

quote: against which this grammar triangulates its analyses
issue: Meta-commentary about the grammar's methodology addresses the production process rather than the reader.
suggestion: Rephrase to 'against which the present grammar is triangulated' or simply 'Together with Tamura's Saru dictionary and grammar, it forms one of the two principal Japanese-language reference works for Ainu.'

quote: large parts of the historical account in the present chapter draw on that survey
issue: Meta-commentary about the chapter's sources addresses the production process rather than the reader.
suggestion: Rephrase to 'The historical account in this chapter draws heavily on that survey.'

quote: held in this project's sources in the <Ref k="batchelor1905" /> edition and in its final revised form of <Ref k="batchelor1938" />
issue: Meta-commentary about source edition addresses the production process rather than the reader.
suggestion: Rephrase to 'His dictionary — in the <Ref k="batchelor1905" /> edition and its final revised form of <Ref k="batchelor1938" /> — combined...'

quote: Refsing identifies two advances in particular: he demonstrated that the language's person-indexing elements are personal affixes on the verb rather than the free pronouns that Batchelor's description had posited, and he distinguished the abstract nominal form from the personal-reference-bearing concrete (possessed) form
issue: Ambiguous pronoun 'he' after 'Refsing' – the antecedent is Kindaichi, not Refsing.
suggestion: Change 'he demonstrated' to 'Kindaichi demonstrated' and 'he distinguished' to 'Kindaichi distinguished' to clarify the antecedent.

quote: marked with the dialect tag sa
issue: Dialect tag uses an HTML span with class 'sc' instead of the guillemet tags (e.g., ‹SA›) described in the briefing as the book's convention.
suggestion: Replace with guillemet tag ‹SA› for consistency with the book's style.

quote: re-edited with translation and grammatical notes by <Ref k="dalcorso2021" /> (line 70) and later re-edited and annotated by <Ref k="dalcorso2021" /> (line 142)
issue: The same citation key 'dalcorso2021' is used for two different re-editions (Piłsudski texts and Murasaki grammar), which may be ambiguous if the reference list has only one entry.
suggestion: Ensure the reference list has a single entry for dalcorso2021 that covers both re-editions, or use distinct keys if they are separate works.
